text.title: django-pytest-parametrize Decision Tree
text.summary: Per test-group: parametrize or keep separate?

decision-tree.root-question: How many near-identical test bodies exist?

branch.conclusion: Do NOT parametrize. Two tests with similar bodies are tolerable; refactor when the third appears.

branch.question: Does control flow differ between cases?

branch.conclusion: Do NOT parametrize. Different branches mean different concepts. Keep separate tests.
Parametrize with descriptive IDs (pytest.param(value, id="name") or ids=[]). Argnames as comma-separated tuple names.
Split into category-named test functions (test_valid_emails, test_invalid_emails). Each function parametrizes ≤ 20 cases.

text.example: 4 tests differing only in (field, bad_value, expected_error) q-count -> 3..20 q-control-flow -> false => conclusion r1: parametrize over (field, value, expected_error); ids ["amount-negative", "amount-non-numeric", "due-date-past", "missing-customer"].
